reasoning.typography: Clean, professional system with good scale contrast. The headline 'Our Future is the Ultimate Project' has weight, but the overall system feels restrained rather than distinctive. Not Inter+Fraunces, but not memorable either.
reasoning.hierarchy: Strong hierarchical clarity — the hero statement dominates, black section creates clear visual break, project grid has clean precedence. One thing per screen principle mostly followed.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Generic consultancy language ('We design Brand Identity for Arts & Culture'). The climate change positioning is specific, but the visual execution could work for any design firm. Swap the words, still works.
reasoning.color: Disciplined palette. Pastels in hero are decorative but restrained. Black section provides strong contrast. Project colors come from work, not arbitrary decoration. Accent usage controlled.
reasoning.density: Confident breathing room in hero, good density in black section text block. Project grid has appropriate tightness. Not uniform padding everywhere — shows intentionality.
reasoning.voice: Classic consultancy structure: hero → manifesto → selected work → CTA. The climate change angle is substantive but the IA is still 'Currently/Selected/CTA' stencil. Doesn't reflect their partnership model or unique structure.

winning_patterns: Strong contrast between light hero and black manifesto section creates visual punctuation
Project grid lets work imagery do the talking without excessive chrome
Climate change positioning gives substantive mission beyond 'we make things pretty'
Restrained use of color — pastels as accent, not decoration
anti_patterns: Hero statement 'We design Brand Identity for Arts & Culture' is fill-in-the-blank consultancy language
IA follows standard agency template rather than reflecting Pentagram's unique partnership structure
Pastel squiggles in hero feel like decorative gestures without purpose
No typographic personality — could be any professional services firm
